objective: Stratégie sur FORMUSDC 1h. Objective Trading Strategy - AORONDA v3 on timeframe with Bollinger Bands and MACD indicators, incorporating a unique filter against false signals (partial RSI reversal) and trading based on majority vote of conflicting signals, using a stop loss (SL) at 5% below the lowest low during uptrends and take profit (TP) at 3% above the highest high during downtrends, with an expected risk management strategy. The objective is to trade on AORONDA v3 token in the timeframe using Bollinger Bands and MACD indicators while incorporating a unique filter against false signals (partial RSI reversal). The trading strategy will also be based on majority vote of conflicting signals. Stop loss (SL) at 5% below the lowest low during uptrends, with take profit (TP) at 3% above the highest high during downtrends. The risk management strategy is expected to have SL at 5% below the lowest low during uptrends and TP at 3% above the highest high during downtrends.

iterations.hypothesis: Ajustement structurel basé sur le diagnostic précédent.

code.validation_error: [SIG001] `for i in range(...)` interdit dans la logique Builder.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses Bollinger Bands, MACD and ATR indicators to identify potential long trades. The entry signal is based on the current price being above the middle Bollinger Band and the MACD signal line crossing above its own histogram. Exit occurs when a new trading signal (opposite of the initial one) appears. If results are poor or mediocre, alternative approaches could be tried such as using other indicators like RSI, Williams %R, Awesome Oscillator or CCI. Further adjustments to parameters may also improve performance.
[Backup plan] In case of negative returns, it is suggested to try different combinations of Bollinger Bands and MACD settings, adjusting the period length for each one, adding a filter against false signals using RSI reversal, and considering other indicators like ATR or VWAP. For mediocre returns, adjustments could be made by fine-tuning parameters such as tightening stop loss levels (SL) and take profit (TP), modifying the signal crossing conditions in order to capture more profitable trades.
